def: s.m. The front parts, pudendum viri vel feminœ.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قبل (qabl), from Arabic قَبْل (qabl).

def: adj. (f. -ī) = kubbā and kubja, q.v. s.m. An arch; a dome, vault, cupola; the saloon of a tomb; an alcove; the ornamental ball on the top of a dome, or a spire, or a flag-staff, &c.
s.m. The major proposition (of a syllogism).
adj. = H کوبڙ कूबड़ kūbaṛ, s.m. (dialec.) = kubaṛ, and kubṛā, qq.v.
adj. Devoid of understanding, stupid, foolish; of depraved mind, vicious, wicked, mischievous; — s.f. Folly, stupidity; depravity, &c.
adj. Distressingly or plaguily heavy.
s.f. pl. (of qabr, q.v.), Graves, tombs; — holsters (for pistols).
s.m. Marching; decamping; march; journey; departure
subs: To make a march or journey, to march; to journey; to decamp, depart; to depart this life
s.m. March and halt, or marches and halts.

def: s.m. A nipple.
s.m. A narrow street, a lane, a narrow passage, an alley
subs: s.f. Marking off the boundaries of a lane
s.f. Perambulation of the streets, wandering about (in a town), strolling.

def: s.m. A breast; the female breast (esp. that of a young unmarried woman; — cf. kuća).
s.m. See s.v. kuć. s.m. A nipple.
s.m. Unrest, uneasiness, restlessness, &c.

ety: prob. = क्षुद्रक; cf. kotāh, and kūdak
def: adj. Small, little, diminutive; younger, junior (syn. ćhoṭā).
s.f. Smallness, diminutiveness; — purple colour.
s.f. Misconduct, misdemeanour, evil course, immorality; — unruliness, restiveneness; — adj. Misbehaved, ill-conducted, &c. ( = ku-ćālī, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Ill-behaved, of bad conduct, of evil or corrupt life, immoral; — crooked, irregular; — an ill-behaved person, &c.

ety: prob. a caus. form. fr. S. कुट्ट; or akin to kaćūrnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To bruise, crush, triturate; to trample on; to overlay; to beat to pulp; — v.n. To be bruised, &c.
subs: or kućal-ḍālnā, v.t. intens. of and = kućalnā.

def: s.m. Red sanders, Pterocarpus santalinus; sappan or logwood, Cœsalpina sappan.
s.f. = ku-ćāl, q.v. s.f. Misconduct, misdemeanour, evil course, immorality; — unruliness, restiveneness; — adj. Misbehaved, ill-conducted, &c. ( = ku-ćālī, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Following evil practices, low, vile, wicked (cf. ku-ćāl); speaking ill (of anyone), detracting, censorious; — a detractor, calumniator; — a fixed star (lit. 'moving slowly').
adj. (f. -ī), Ill-clothed, dressed in old or dirty or tattered garments, ragged, tattered (com. used in combination with mailā, e.g. mailā-kućailā kapṛā).
s.m. A bad garment, inferior raiment; coarse cloth; — adj. = next, q.v.
v.t. = kućalnā, q.v. v.t. To bruise, crush, triturate; to trample on; to overlay; to beat to pulp; — v.n. To be bruised, &c.
subs: or kućal-ḍālnā, v.t. intens. of and = kućalnā.

ety: from old H. कछू = S. कच्चित् (akin to किंचित्)
def: pron. & adv. Something, somewhat, anything, aught; some, any; a little, a few; ever so little; whatever; in any manner or degree, at all
subs: Somewhat more; something additional; something different, a very different tale or account; a false account or explanation
Somewhat, some little, a little, some few
It is nothing, it is a matter of no consequence, there is nothing in this; (this) is nothing to the purpose, (this) is no reason at all; (this) is no excuse, (this) will not do or answer
It is of no consequence, no matter, never mind, nʼimporte
Have you found something lying (in your path)ʼ? have you found a treasure? (addressed to one who seems inordinately pleased without apparent cause)

'What! have you dreamt something?' or What! have you seen a vision? Are you in your senses? (addressed to a person who relates improbabilities; or used to express the astonishment of the speaker at any unexpected act of another's)
There is no fear, or there is nothing to fear, do not be alarmed! it does not matter, no matter, never mind
Something else, something quite different; (in) another manner; on the contrary; — a great advance or increase (on)
or kućh-se kućh ho jānā, To become quite different, to be or become transformed, to be or become entirely changed
Some little, a little, somewhat; rather; tolerably
v.t. To make something else of, to transform; to bewitch, charm
or kućh kah-baiṭhnā (-se), To say something unpleasant (to)
Some at least, somewhat; something or other
Nothing, not any, none; it is nothing at all; it is of no account
Whatever may be, happen or come what may; — kućh ho-jānā, To be quite changed, to be completely transformed; to be possessed by an evil spirit
Everything, the whole, all that was possible

def: s.m. A tuft; a cluster.
s.f. A brush (for painting, or plastering, or white-washing); a hair-pencil; a weaver's brush; a pointed or forked beard
subs: (-par), or kūćī karnā (-ko), To pass the brush (over), to brush; to plaster, to whitewash.

def: s.f. A brush, &c. ( = kūćī, q.v.). s.f. A brush (for painting, or plastering, or white-washing); a hair-pencil; a weaver's brush; a pointed or forked beard
subs: (-par), or kūćī karnā (-ko), To pass the brush (over), to brush; to plaster, to whitewash.

def: s.m. A dialec. form of kućlā, q.v.
s.m. The lobe of the ear ( = kūćiyā).
s.f. A small tamarind; — lobe of the ear ( = kućiyā).
s.f. A species of bird, the red-headed Trogon.

ety: prob. S. कुच, q.v. + ल+कः; cf. S. कुचफल 'a pomegranate'
def: s.m. Vomic nut, Strychnos nux-vomica (a small evergreen tree, the berry or fruit of which is of the size and colour of an orange).
part. adj. (f. -ī), Bruised, crushed, triturated
s.f. The act of crushing, crunching; trituration.
s.f. A canine tooth, an eye-tooth; a grinder.
v.t. a dialec. var. of kūṅćnā, q.v.
s.f. Springing, jumping, &c.; a spring, jump, leap, bound
subs: s.f. Leaping and jumping; skipping and dancing about; gambols; capers
To take a leap, to jump, &c.

def: s.f. Jumping (into water, &c.); reward or present given for jumping.

ety: Pehl. kūdak; Zend kutaka; S. क्षुद्रक
def: s.m. A boy, lad, youth, youngster, stripling (Pers. pl. kūdakān).

ety: kudak˚ = Prk. कुंदक्के(इ), fr. S. स्कुन्द्+कृ
def: v.n. To leap, jump; to frisk, caper, skip; to gambol.
s.f. A small mattock, a pickaxe; a dibble, &c. (see kudāl, of which it is the dim.).
pron. Who? which? (particular one, of two or more, — syn. kaun-sā); what?
s.m. pl. (of qadīm, q.v.), The ancients.
prop. کدانا kudānā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to jump or leap; to dandle (a child); (met.) to provoke.
s.m. = kudāl, q.v.
s.f. = kudālī, q.v. s.f. A small mattock, a pickaxe; a dibble, &c. (see kudāl, of which it is the dim.).
s.f. Bad state, low condition, miserable plight, misery.
adj. Ill-formed, ill-shaped, ungainly, ugly (cf. ku-ḍhab).

ety: i.q. kudāʼī, with S. इकः instead of इका
def: s.m. A dove; a pigeon (prob. so called from its 'leaping' when cooing to the female).
s.m. Mountain ebony, Bauhinia variegata.
s.m. Mountain ebony ( = kuddāl).
s.m. A kind of hoe or spade; a pickaxe, a mattock.
adj. Pure, holy, blessed; — an epithet of the Deity.
adj. Ill-shaped, ungainly, ugly; ill-behaved, ill-mannered, unmannerly, ill-bred, rude, uncouth; — ku-ḍhab, and ku-ḍhaṅg, s.m. Misbehaviour, bad manners; misconduct, evil course.
adj. Ill-shaped, ungainly, ugly; ill-behaved, ill-mannered, unmannerly, ill-bred, rude, uncouth; — ku-ḍhab, and ku-ḍhaṅg, s.m. Misbehaviour, bad manners; misconduct, evil course.
adj. Ill-shaped, ungainly, ugly; ill-behaved, ill-mannered, unmannerly, ill-bred, rude, uncouth; — ku-ḍhab, and ku-ḍhaṅg, s.m. Misbehaviour, bad manners; misconduct, evil course.
adj. Ill-shaped, ungainly, ugly; ill-behaved, ill-mannered, unmannerly, ill-bred, rude, uncouth; — ku-ḍhab, and ku-ḍhaṅg, s.m. Misbehaviour, bad manners; misconduct, evil course.
s.f. Misbehaviour, unmannerliness, rudeness.
s.m. A mountain.
s.m. Iron.
adj. & s.m. Foolish; stupid; — a fool.
s.f. Bad state, low condition, miserable plight, misery.

ety: kūd˚ = Prk. कुंद(इ) = S. स्कुन्द(ते), rt. स्कुन्द्
def: v.n. To leap, jump, spring, bound; to hop; to hobble; — (met.) to rejoice; to boast; to presume (upon, ūpar)
subs: v.n. To jump or leap about; to jump or skip for joy; to gambol
To go by jumps or hops; to hop; to hobble.

def: s.m. = kudāl, q.v.
v.t. = kudlānā, q.v.; — v.n. To leap, to frisk, to caper ( = kūdnā).

ety: for A. قدرة, inf. n. of قدر 'to be able,' &c.
def: s.f. Power, ability, potency, vigour, force, authority, virtue; divine power, omnipotence; — the creation, the universe, nature
subs: (-kī), To have power (to), to be able.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قُدْرَت (qudrat), borrowed from Arabic قُدْرَة (qudra), from قَدَرَ (qadara). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi قدرت (qdrt /⁠qudrat⁠/).

def: adj. Of omnipotence, divine; not made or produced by man, natural, constitutional, innate; instinctive
subs: adj. & s.m. Of (its) natural colour; white; — natural colour (not stained or dyed)

def: s.f. Weak sight, defective vision; evil eye; ill-intended look; lascivious glance; — a false system.
s.f. Weak sight, defective vision; evil eye; ill-intended look; lascivious glance; — a false system.

ety: inf. n. of قدس 'to be pure, or holy'
def: adj. Pure, holy; — s.m. Holiness, sanctity; — the angel Gabriel; — the holy city, Jerusalem.
adj. & s.m. Holy; divine; celestial; — the angel Gabriel.

ety: for A. قدسية qudsīyat, fem. of qudsī
def: adj. Holy, &c. ( = qudsī, q.v.). adj. & s.m. Holy; divine; celestial; — the angel Gabriel.
s.m. pl. (of قدّ qadd), Goat-skins.

ety: inf. n. of قدم 'to come near,' &c.
def: s.m. Arrival, coming near, approach; accession; advent.

(inf. n. of کدر), s.m.(?) = kudūrat, q.v. s.f. Muddiness, turbidness; impurity (in water, &c.); foulness; scum; dust; — (met.) perturbation, depression of spirits; affliction, anguish; vexation; discord; resentment, malice
subs: (dil-meṅ), To harbour resentment; to bear malice.

ety: for A. کدورة, inf. n. of کدر 'to be turbid,' &c.
def: s.f. Muddiness, turbidness; impurity (in water, &c.); foulness; scum; dust; — (met.) perturbation, depression of spirits; affliction, anguish; vexation; discord; resentment, malice
subs: (dil-meṅ), To harbour resentment; to bear malice.

def: v.t. To cause to leap; to cause to dandle; to cause to be dandled.
s.m. A narrow street, a lane, a turn-again lane ( = kū).
s.f. & adj. contrac. of kufū, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of kāfir), Infidels; idolaters, pagans.

ety: prob. corr. fr. P. کوپله kūpala or kaupala
def: s.m. A padlock, a lock; a bolt
subs: (-kā), To break open the lock (of); to force the door (of a house)
or qufl lag̠ānā (-meṅ), To fasten (a thing) with a padlock, to pat a lock (on); to lock (up or in)

def: s.f. A mould (for ice, jelly, &c.); — a small saucer-shaped earthen vessel; — qufliyāṅ, s.f. pl. Small saucer-shaped earthen vessels fitted with khīr (which are commonly interchanged as presents by families between which a marriage is arranged, from the first to the fortieth day after Moḥarram).

ety: inf. n. of کفر 'to be unbelieving, or ungrateful,' &c.
def: s.m. Unbelief, infidelity; incredulity; scepticism; ingratitude; paganism, idolatry; impiety, blasphemy
subs: or kufr-kā kalma muṅh-se nikālnā, To utter blasphemous language; to talk nonsense, to prate
(-kā), To overcome the infidelity (of), to make a convert to Islam (of); to overcome the obstinacy (of); to break the resolution (of a person)
s.m. Land of infidelity, country of infidels, &c.; a mountainous region to the north-east of Kābul
s.f. Evil company, company where blasphemy and ribaldry, &c. is heard; an obscene play (resembling the English 'Judge and Jury') performed during the Holī festival

ety: inf. n. of کفر; see kufr
def: s.m. Unbelief; ingratitude ( = kufr)
adj. & s.m. Infidel, pagan; — an infidel, a pagan, a Caffre (generally applied to a barbarous race inhabiting the south-east coast of Africa).
s.f. A sprain; a bruise; a rupture, &c.
part. Sprained, bruised, &c.

ety: for A. kufwuṉ, or kufʼuṉ, v.n. fr. کفأ
def: adj. & s.m. Alike, equal, resembling; of the same kind or class or tribe; — tribe, caste, family, brotherhood.
s.f. A bad odour, offensive smell, stench.
s.m. An infirm bull.
s.m. contrac. of koh, q.v.
subs: q.v. adj. & s.m. See s.v. koh. s.m. A mountain, hill, hillock
A snowy mountain, or snowy mountains
adj. 'A mountain in form,' mountain-like; huge, bulky
or koh-ě-ravanda, s.m. 'A moving mountain or mass,' an elephant
adj. & s.m. Mountainous, hilly; — a mountainous or hilly country; — a mountain
or koh-qāf, Mount Caucasus; (met.) a dreary or lonely or inaccessible place
s.m. Mountain-digger; an epithet of Farhād
s.f. Digging or cutting through a mountain (or mountains)
adj. Having a huge hump (a camel, or bull).
ex: مُجھے پَتَہ نَہِیں تھا کِہ تُمہیں سِندھِی آتِی ہَے۔
mujhe pata nahī̃ thā ki tumhẽ sindhī ātī hai.
I didn't know that you speak Sindhi.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کِه (ki), from Middle Persian 𐭠𐭩𐭪 (AYK /⁠kū⁠/).

def: s.f. Mist, fog, vapour.

adj. (dialec. or rustic) = ku-ḍhab, q.v. adj. Ill-shaped, ungainly, ugly; ill-behaved, ill-mannered, unmannerly, ill-bred, rude, uncouth; — ku-ḍhab, and ku-ḍhaṅg, s.m. Misbehaviour, bad manners; misconduct, evil course.
s.m. Cheating, fraud, deception, delusion; juggling, sleight-of-hand, legerdemain.
adj. Old, ancient
subs: adj. Advanced in years, old, aged

def: adj. Rough, rugged, uneven (a road or path).
s.m. Cavity, hollow; hole, rent; chasm, abyss.
wety: From Classical Persian کَهَر (kahar, “light brown; bay”).

def: s.m. = kuhrā = kūhrā, q.v. Fog, mist, haze, &c. (cf. kuherī).
wety: From Classical Persian کَهَر (kahar, “light brown; bay”).

def: s.m. Vapour, mist, fog (syn. kohar, kāhrā, kuheṛī, &c.).
s.m. = kuhāsā, or kohar, qq.v. s.m. Vapour, mist, fog (syn. kohar, kāhrā, kuheṛī, &c.).
s.f. = kūhā, 'fog,' q.v. s.f. Mist, fog, vapour.
s.f. = kūhī, q.v. s.f. Mist, fog, vapour.

ety: prob. fr. kūh, onomat.; cf. S. kuhaka-svara
def: s.f. A species of kawk, Falco cristatellus (syn. bairī).
s.m. contract. of kohistān, q.v.
adj. Old, ancient; inveterate; chronic.
s.f. Old age; oldness.

ety: kūhā, q.v.+Prk. डी and डओ = S. री and र +कः
def: Fog, mist, haze, &c. (cf. kuherī).
s.m. = kūhrā or kohar, q.v. Fog, mist, haze, &c. (cf. kuherī).
s.f. Mist, haze, fog, vapour.
contrac. of koh-sār, q.v.s.v. koh.
s.f. = kuhuk, q.v. s.f. Cry (of the koʼil or Indian cuckoo); scream (of the peacock).
s.f. Cry (of the koʼil or Indian cuckoo); scream (of the peacock).
v.n. To utter a shrill cry, to cry (as the koʼil does); to scream, shriek (as the peacock, &c.).
v.n. = kuhuknā, q.v. v.n. To utter a shrill cry, to cry (as the koʼil does); to scream, shriek (as the peacock, &c.).
s.f. = kūhī, 'hawk,' q.v. s.f. Mist, fog, vapour.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit, from Sanskrit कश्चिद् (káścid). Likely also influenced by Sanskrit कोऽपि (kó’pi). Cognate to Awadhi कोई (koī), Braj कोऊ (koū), Punjabi ਕੋਈ (koī), Gujarati કોઈ (koī), Marathi कुणी (kuṇī), Bengali কেউ (keu), Bhojpuri केऊ (kēū), Assamese কেও (keü).

def: s.f. (Dakkhinī) A ring worn as an ornament round the toe (syn. bićhuʼā).
s.m. The planet Mars; Tuesday.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کج (kaj, “crooked”).

ety: ku = Zend ku = S. कु+jā, q.v.
def: adv. Where? whither? how? — az kujā, From where? whence? — har-kujā, adv. Everywhere.
s.m. corr. of kūza, q.v. s.m. An earthen water-bottle with a long narrow neck, a gugglet; a pitcher; a jug; a jar; — sugar crystallized in an earthen pot or pan; — a kind of rose
subs: 'A river (or the sea) in a jug'; much in little, multum in parvo.

def: s.f. A bad mother.
adj. & s.m. (f. ā), Low-born, of mean extraction, of low or bad caste; low, base; — one who has lost his caste, an outcast; — ku-jāti, s.f. Meanness of origin or birth, low extraction.
adj. & s.m. (f. ā), Low-born, of mean extraction, of low or bad caste; low, base; — one who has lost his caste, an outcast; — ku-jāti, s.f. Meanness of origin or birth, low extraction.
s.m. A forest frequented by elephants.
v.n. To ferment, to rot, putrefy, to become stinking or rotten; to become mouldy or musty; to moulder away, to become old, to decay.
v.n. (dialec.) To speak, &c.
s.m. A bad or wicked ascetic; a false devotee; a magician, a juggler.
s.m. A bad or wicked ascetic; a false devotee; a magician, a juggler.
s.f. Crying; sobbing, wailing; cry, scream, shriek (of a human being, or of a peacock, or cuckoo, &c.); a warble, a trill
subs: or kūk mārnā, To cry, sob, whine, wail, moan; to utter a shrill cry, to scream (as a peacock, &c.), to shriek; to shout, to halloo.

def: s.f. Voice, cry; barking, bark (of a dog)
s.f. Winding (a skein, or a ball of thread, &c.), winding up (a clock or watch, &c.); tuning (a musical instrument).
s.m. A sect of Sikhs, followers of Rām Singh (so called because they used to shriek at their nocturnal meetings).
s.f. Sobbing, crying, whining, screaming, shrieking.
s.f. Evil desire or inclination; concupiscence.
s.m. = kukur or kukkur, q.v.

ety: Prk. कुक्कुरु, or कुक्कुरो; S. कुक्कुरः
def: s.m. A dog (see kukur)
subs: s.m. 'A dog's sojourn'; a short stay, a flying visit
s.m. A small wild tree with thick leaves (a plaster made of which is considered a specific for the bite of a mad dog)
s.m. A fungus (growing on wood); a toadstool.

def: v.t. To cover, to overlay.
s.m. Bad action, evil-doing. misconduct, villainy, vice, sin; fornication
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Maleficent, profligate, depraved, wicked, sinful; — evil-doer, malefactor, profligate; fornicator, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. = ku-karm-kārī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = ku-karm-kārī, q.v.
s.f. The crow of a cock, cock-a-doodle-do.
s.f. Evil report, infamy, disrepute.
s.m. (dialec.) A dog ( = kukar, kuttā)
s.m. (f. -ī), A cock; a male bird.
s.m. A dog ( = kuttā); — (met.) a Kshatriya; — name of a certain prince; name of a people (a branch of the Yādavas)
subs: s.f. lit. 'Dog-cough'; a barking cough, a bad cough
wety: Borrowed from English cooker.

def: s.f. A bitch; (met.) a female Kshatriya (see kukur).
s.m. A cock, a wild cock, Phasianus gallus; a male bird; a claw
subs: s.m. A religious observance (worship of Śiva) on the seventh day of the light fortnight of the month Bhādoṅ, by women (especially for the sake of obtaining offspring).

def: s.f. A hen; — the silk-cotton tree, Bombax heptaphyllon.

ety: kūk˚ = Prk. कुक्क(इ), or कुक्के(इ); fr. S. कु 'to cry'+कृ
def: v.n. To cry, pule, whine, sob, moan, lament; to utter a shrill cry, to cry (as the kokil or Indian cuckoo), to scream (as the peacock, &c.); to call, to vociferate, to halloo.

ety: prob. fr. S. क्रुक्त 'curved,' &c.; rt. क्रुञ्च्
def: v.t. To wind, to twist, to form into hanks or skeins (see kukṛī); to wind up (a watch, or a musical box, &c.); to tune (a musical instrument).
s.m. = ققنوس quqnūs, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī), A cock; a male bird.
s.f. = kukṛī, q.v.
s.f. A hen.

ety: kūk(nā), 'to wind' + ṛī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A clew, hank, or skein (of string or thread); a ball of thread on a spindle; thread knotted or twisted when on the spindle; — a curved knife (peculiar to Nepal); — a cob (of Indian corn); seed-vessel (of maize or of āk)
subs: v.n. To curl up, to become bent or curved; to shrink, contract

def: s.f. A bitch ( = kutīyā).
s.f. A skein, or hank, &c. ( = kukri, or kukṛī, q.v.); — 2˚ = kurkurī, q.v.
s.f. (Dakkhinī) = kukṛī, q.v.
s.f. A bad action, &c. ( = ku-karm, q.v.). s.m. Bad action, evil-doing. misconduct, villainy, vice, sin; fornication
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Maleficent, profligate, depraved, wicked, sinful; — evil-doer, malefactor, profligate; fornicator, &c.

def: s.m. contrac. of next, q.v.
s.m. The belly, cavity of the abdomen; the womb (see kokh); a cavity; cavern; a bay.
s.f. Cooing (of a dove); a dove.
s.m. A fried egg; an omelet
s.m. A kind of disease of the eyes, weakness of the eyes (in infants).
s.m. = kukuroṅdā, q.v.
s.m. The plant Celsia, and its fruit.

ety: imperat. of قال for قول 'to say'
def: s.m. The first word of sūras 112, 113, and 114 of the Qorʼān (beginning respectively with the verses qul, huwaʼllāhǒ aḥaduṉ, 'Say, God is One'; qul, aʻūẕu bi-rabbiʼl-falaqi, 'Say, I seek the protection of the Lord of the Daybreak'; and qul, aʻūẕu bi-rabbiʼn-nāsi, 'Say, I seek the protection of the Lord of Men'), and used to denote one and all of these sūras or chapters; — reading any one or all of the above-mentioned sūras (this is commonly done at a fātiḥa, q.v., or, by way of benediction, over fruits or sweetmeats presented to the guest at the conclusion of the religious annual feasts held in commemoration of ancestors and other relations); — (hence) conclusion, finish, end
subs: (fr. qul, aʻūẕu, &c.; see above), s.m. One whose practice it is to recite the sūras beginning with the words qul, aʻūẕu for a remuneration in money or food
A fair held in commemoration of the death of Moḥammad; — a spiritualistic gathering, or kind of revival, at which men caper and dance and roll on the ground in a state of religious frenzy
v.n. To be concluded, to come to an end; to be all over
v.n. To be finished; to be worn or tired out; to be brought to the verge of death or destruction; to come to an end, to die
'To repeat the words qul, huwaʼllāh, &c., or Say, God is One'; — to grumble (the bowels; see s.v. aṅtrī); to be hungry.

ety: Ap. Prk. कुलु; Prk. कुलं or कुलो; S. कुलं
def: s.m. A herd, troop, flock, assemblage, multitude; a race, caste, tribe, family, house, relations; lineage, pedigree; noble or eminent family or race, noble lineage; — a grange holding as much land as can be tilled by two ploughs each drawn by six bulls
subs: (la+āć˚), s.m. The peculiar or proper duty of a family, or caste, or race ( = kul-dharm)
(˚la+āć˚), s.m. A family teacher, family priest; one well versed in the pedigrees and customs of different families and employed to contract marriages between them
(˚la+ar˚), and kul-ujāgar, s.m. An epithet of anyone who occasions the celebrity of his family or tribe (e.g. Rām; and Christ, of the tribe of Judah, &c.)
s.f. A virtuous woman of a noble family
(f. -ā), or kul-boṛak (f. -ikā), s.m. One who disgraces his family; a reprobate
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Protecting a family, nourishing or providing for a family; — one who nourishes or provides for a family
s.f. A chaste, high-born, or noble woman
s.m. The head or chief of a family or race; a patriarch
s.m. Friends and relatives of the same tribe or caste, family connexions, kith and kin
or kul-pūjya, or kul-pūjak, s.m. The object of worship or reverence in a family, a household god; a family priest
s.m. The saviour of his family or tribe; a youth who is a credit to his family
or kul-drohī, s.m. (f. -iṇī), One who brings disgrace or injury on his own family
s.m. Practice or observance peculiar to a caste, tribe, or family; peculiar or proper duty of a family or caste or race
s.f. A principal deity; an epithet of the goddess Durgā
s.f. Family goddess, any female deity worshipped in particular by a family through successive generations
adj. (f. -ī), Of or relating to a caste or family; in respect of caste or family
s.m. (f. -inī), One who disgraces a family
s.f. Abuse of one's own family or tribe
vulg. kul-marjād, s.f. Family honour or respectability; — also = kul-dharm, q.v.
s.m. A reprobate; an outcast; — a camel
s.f. A girl worshipped at the celebration of the orgies of the left-hand Śāktas
or kul-vat, or kul-vant, adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Of noble family or descent, of gentle blood, well-born, genteel, pure, chaste; — a person of good family, &c.
s.f. A gentlewoman, a respectable or noble woman; a virtuous woman.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.m. Declivity, slope; bank (of a river, &c.), shore, margin, edge, side; a heap, mound; a pool, pond, lake.
s.m. The hip, buttock, haunch; the waist.

ety: for A. قلابة qulābat, fr. qalb; see qullāb; cf. also kalāba
def: s.m. A hook; a hasp; a staple; a link; a hinge; handle (of a cup, &c.).

s.m. See s.v. kul. s.m. The first word of sūras 112, 113, and 114 of the Qorʼān (beginning respectively with the verses qul, huwaʼllāhǒ aḥaduṉ, 'Say, God is One'; qul, aʻūẕu bi-rabbiʼl-falaqi, 'Say, I seek the protection of the Lord of the Daybreak'; and qul, aʻūẕu bi-rabbiʼn-nāsi, 'Say, I seek the protection of the Lord of Men'), and used to denote one and all of these sūras or chapters; — reading any one or all of the above-mentioned sūras (this is commonly done at a fātiḥa, q.v., or, by way of benediction, over fruits or sweetmeats presented to the guest at the conclusion of the religious annual feasts held in commemoration of ancestors and other relations); — (hence) conclusion, finish, end
subs: (fr. qul, aʻūẕu, &c.; see above), s.m. One whose practice it is to recite the sūras beginning with the words qul, aʻūẕu for a remuneration in money or food
A fair held in commemoration of the death of Moḥammad; — a spiritualistic gathering, or kind of revival, at which men caper and dance and roll on the ground in a state of religious frenzy
v.n. To be concluded, to come to an end; to be all over
v.n. To be finished; to be worn or tired out; to be brought to the verge of death or destruction; to come to an end, to die
'To repeat the words qul, huwaʼllāh, &c., or Say, God is One'; — to grumble (the bowels; see s.v. aṅtrī); to be hungry.

def: s.m. = H کلچهنا कुलच्छणा ku-laććhaṇā, adj. See ku-lakshan, and ku-lakshaṇā.
adj. = H کلچهن कुलच्छण ku-laććhaṇ, s.m. See ku-lakshan, and ku-lakshaṇā.
s.f. A cap, hat, bonnet; cowl; tiara, crown; mitre.
s.m. Cap, hat, &c.
subs: adj. Wearing a hat or cap
(vulg. -shajra), s.m. Succession and property of a saint, or a faqīr, &c.

def: s.m. = kolāhal, q.v. s.m. A loud and confused sound, a great and indistinct noise (of men, or animals, &c.), screaming, shouting, uproar, tumult, din, hubbub, clatter; mirth, revelry, riot
subs: To set up a scream, to make an uproar, &c.

def: s.m. The plant Coryza terebinthina, or Celsia coromandelina (a plant which dogs are fond of smelling before they eject urine).

s.m. Bad sign, unlucky mark; a mark, sign, appearance, quality, or mental feature that bodes evil; evil omen; ugly visage; bad temper; misconduct; — adj. (f. -ā), Having bad or ill-boding marks, or signs, &c.; of evil omen, ominous; forbidding (appearance), ugly; ill-tempered
subs: s.f. A woman of forbidding appearance, or of bad temper, or evil disposition.

def: s.m. Bad sign, unlucky mark; a mark, sign, appearance, quality, or mental feature that bodes evil; evil omen; ugly visage; bad temper; misconduct; — adj. (f. -ā), Having bad or ill-boding marks, or signs, &c.; of evil omen, ominous; forbidding (appearance), ugly; ill-tempered
subs: s.f. A woman of forbidding appearance, or of bad temper, or evil disposition.

def: adj. (f. -ī) = ku-lakshaṇ.
s.m. A potter.
s.f. = kulāṅć, q.v.
s.f. Bound, spring, leap, jump, bounce
s.m. The coolen or crane, Ardea sibirica; — a fowl of a large and good breed.
s.m. Colic.
adj. Cutting behind in walking (a horse); — knock-kneed (a man).
s.m. The plant Alpinia galanga, or Galanga major; the seed Nigella sativa; — the root of the Piper betel (cf. kholinjān).
s.f. An unchaste woman, a harlot.
s.f. Red arsenic.
s.f. A blue stone used in medicine and applied as a collyrium to the eyes, also as an astringent to sores, &c.
adj. = kul-vant, q.v.s.v. kul.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit कलावत् (kalāvat).

def: s.m. A plough
s.m. House, cottage, hut, closet, cell
subs: House of affliction or grief, an afflicted or distressed family.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کلبه (kulba).

def: adj. (f. ī), Fidgety, restless; wriggling (as a worm, &c.); vermicular, peristaltic.
s.f. The act of writhing, wriggling, &c.; peristaltic or vermicular motion; titillation, itchiness.

v.n. To flutter; to be in a flutter; to toss about (in pain, &c., murmuring at the same time); to fidget; to writhe, wriggle (as a worm, &c.); — to bestir itself (a thought, &c.) in the mind, to occur to the mind; — to be in a state of titillation, to itch; — to grumble, rumble (the bowels).
s.m. Capital, principal, stock in trade.
s.m. A small disc-shaped loaf, a cake of bread (made of flour, milk, and butter); a kind of sweetmeat; a circular piece of wood which rests on a tent-pole near its upper extremity.

ety: prob. a dialec. form of kalol
def: s.f. Friskiness, capering, restiveness (of a horse).
s.m. corr. of ḵẖurfa, q.v. s.m. That which is plucked from a tree, gathered or plucked fruits; autumnal fruit.

ety: for A. کلفة, fr. kalaf, q.v.; & cf. taklīf
def: s.f. Trouble, vexation, distress, inconvenience.

s.f. A small ḥuqqa-snake; a curved piece of a ḥuqqa-snake (made of copper); — a small saucer-shaped earthen vessel (see quflī); a cup with a cover (in which ice is moulded); a mould (for ices, jellies, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قفلی (quflī, “mould”), from قفل (qufl, “lock”), from Arabic قُفْل (qufl, “lock, bolt”).

def: s.m. = kūlā, q.v. s.m. The hip, buttock, haunch; the waist.
s.m. A kind of firework, a 'flower-pot.'

ety: Prk. कुढालओ, and कुढालिआ; S. कुठारकः and कुठारिका
def: s.f. (dim.) [Prk. कुढालओ, and कुढालिआ; S. कुठारकः and कुठारिका],ʻAn axe, a hatchet.

ety: Prk. कुढालओ, and कुढालिआ; S. कुठारकः and कुठारिका
def: s.f. (dim.) = H کلهاڙا कुल्हाड़ा kulhāṛā, s.m. [Prk. कुढालओ, and कुढालिआ; S. कुठारकः and कुठारिका],ʻAn axe, a hatchet.
adj. Like a hatchet, hatchet-shaped.
s.f. A small earthen cup; a small earthen (saucer-shaped) vessel; a cup (for cupping); a crucible; a kind of fire-work (cf. kulhaṛ)
subs: (-ko), To offer cups full of milk or sherbet (to a goddess); to fill kulhiyās with parched rice (in the Diwālī festival)
'To break guṛ or sugar in a small cup'; to endeavour to do with few hands a work which requires many, to attempt (or to do) something impossible; to do a thing secretly.

ety: a dialec. form of kuṛhnā, q.v.
def: v.n. To fret, to moan, groan, &c.

ety: S. कुट+dim. aff. ṛā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. A cup, an earthen vessel to drink out of ( = kulṛā; cf. kulhiyā).
s.m. A slave; a coolie, labourer, porter.
wety: From the possessed form of Old Anatolian Turkish قل (“slave”), ultimately from Proto-Turkic *kul; used in several names of the Qutb Shahi sultans.

def: s.m. The hand.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀺𑀕 (kaliga), from Sanskrit कलिका (kalikā). Cognate with Gujarati કળી (kaḷī), Marathi कळी (kaḷī).

def: s.m. = kūlī, corr. of qulī, q.v. s.m. A slave; a coolie, labourer, porter.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀺𑀕 (kaliga), from Sanskrit कलिका (kalikā). Cognate with Gujarati કળી (kaḷī), Marathi कळी (kaḷī).

def: s.f. A tambourine.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀺𑀕 (kaliga), from Sanskrit कलिका (kalikā). Cognate with Gujarati કળી (kaḷī), Marathi कळी (kaḷī).

def: s.m. A coolie, labourer, porter, carrier.

ety: dim. of kulī, which is prob. akin to S. गुली, and गोल
def: s.m. Orb or disk (of the sun or moon); a cake (of bread), &c. (see kulća).
s.m. = kulanjan, q.v. s.m. The plant Alpinia galanga, or Galanga major; the seed Nigella sativa; — the root of the Piper betel (cf. kholinjān).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Of noble descent, of high birth, of good family, well-born; — a nobleman; a man of good family; (in Bengal) a Brāhman of the highest class
subs: s.f. A gentlewoman, a woman of good family.

def: s.m. A kind of wild kidney-bean; a kind of pickle.
Nobility of descent, gentility; birth, rank, family respectability.
Nobility of descent, gentility; birth, rank, family respectability.
Nobility of descent, gentility; birth, rank, family respectability.
s.m. A kind of mouse; — the fork-tailed shrike.
s.m. = qūlinj, q.v.
1˚ = kulanj; 2˚ = kuling, qq.v. adj. Cutting behind in walking (a horse); — knock-kneed (a man).
s.m. = کلش kuliś, q.v.
s.m. An axe, a hatchet; — the thunderbolt of Indra.
s.f. = kulhiyā, q.v. s.f. A small earthen cup; a small earthen (saucer-shaped) vessel; a cup (for cupping); a crucible; a kind of fire-work (cf. kulhaṛ)
subs: (-ko), To offer cups full of milk or sherbet (to a goddess); to fill kulhiyās with parched rice (in the Diwālī festival)
'To break guṛ or sugar in a small cup'; to endeavour to do with few hands a work which requires many, to attempt (or to do) something impossible; to do a thing secretly.

def: s.f. The noise made by water in the neck of a bottle when pouring out, gurgling.
s.m. Gargling (see kullā, kullī); — a kind of ḥuqqa.
s.m. A ḥuqqa.

ety: prob. a var. form of kilkilanā
def: v.n. To laugh aloud ( = khilkhilānā); to giggle, titter; — to itch; to be restless or fidgety (i.q. kulbulānā).
s.f. Giggling; — itching; itch (the disease); — uneasiness, restlessness, fidgetiness.
adj. See s.v. kull. s.f. & adv. Sum total, full amount; — in the total, in all
subs: s.m. The whole case, all the facts of the case, full particulars
s.m. Sole and entire proprietor; one entrusted with the whole management of a house, factotum, major domo
adj. with or according to the whole (assessment, &c.)
All of them, the whole; in all, altogether, wholly, totally

def: s.f. & adv. Sum total, full amount; — in the total, in all
subs: s.m. The whole case, all the facts of the case, full particulars
s.m. Sole and entire proprietor; one entrusted with the whole management of a house, factotum, major domo
adj. with or according to the whole (assessment, &c.)
All of them, the whole; in all, altogether, wholly, totally
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.m. Gargling, rinsing or washing the mouth
subs: To gargle, to rinse the mouth.
wety: From Sanskrit कील (kīl).

ety: for A. قلة qullat, v.n. fr. قلّ 'to diminish,' &c.
def: s.m. Summit (of a mountain), top, or head (of anything).
s.m. A hook; a pot-hook; a hooked instrument ( = qullāb).

ety: prob. a recent formation fr. A. قلب qalb, 'inverting, turning over,' &c.; or = kullāb
def: s.m. A hook (to hang anything on); a pot-hook; a fish-hook; a hooked instrument for drawing bread from ovens; an iron goad, hooked at one end, used by elephant-drivers; — the hood put on a hawk (to blind him); — drawing and relaxing the string of a bow repeatedly before discharging the arrow.

ety: obl. dual of qullat, 'a large jar,' fr. قلّ
def: s.m. (orig.) 'Two jars containing in all twelve hundred pints of water' (this quantity being considered as sufficient to make a dirty person clean); — a warm bath; a cup, or jar, which is in common use by many; — a common prostitute; — adj. In common use by many, touched by many, impure.
s.f. Gargling; a gargle; rinsing or washing the mouth; a mouthful (of any liquid)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀺𑀕 (kaliga), from Sanskrit कलिका (kalikā). Cognate with Gujarati કળી (kaḷī), Marathi कळी (kaḷī).

def: adj. Universal, all, entire, total; every; complete; general, common, generic; — s.f. Total sum, the whole.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀺𑀕 (kaliga), from Sanskrit कलिका (kalikā). Cognate with Gujarati કળી (kaḷī), Marathi कळी (kaḷī).

def: adj. & s.m. = kullīyat, q.v. s.f. pl. (of kullīyat), The whole, everything; the complete works (of an author).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کلیه (kulliyya), from Arabic كُلِّيَّة (kulliyya).

ety: for A. کلية, fem. of kullī
def: s.f. Universality, totality, the whole.
s.f. pl. (of kullīyat), The whole, everything; the complete works (of an author).
s.m. Any food stuffed into the intestines of animals; a sausage; black-pudding; haggis.
s.m. Any food stuffed into intestines; a sausage; black-pudding; haggis.
v.n. = kūlhnā, q.v. v.n. To fret, to moan, groan, &c.
s.m. = kulhṛā, q.v. s.m. A cup, an earthen vessel to drink out of ( = kulṛā; cf. kulhiyā).

s.m. See s.v. kul. s.m. The first word of sūras 112, 113, and 114 of the Qorʼān (beginning respectively with the verses qul, huwaʼllāhǒ aḥaduṉ, 'Say, God is One'; qul, aʻūẕu bi-rabbiʼl-falaqi, 'Say, I seek the protection of the Lord of the Daybreak'; and qul, aʻūẕu bi-rabbiʼn-nāsi, 'Say, I seek the protection of the Lord of Men'), and used to denote one and all of these sūras or chapters; — reading any one or all of the above-mentioned sūras (this is commonly done at a fātiḥa, q.v., or, by way of benediction, over fruits or sweetmeats presented to the guest at the conclusion of the religious annual feasts held in commemoration of ancestors and other relations); — (hence) conclusion, finish, end
subs: (fr. qul, aʻūẕu, &c.; see above), s.m. One whose practice it is to recite the sūras beginning with the words qul, aʻūẕu for a remuneration in money or food
A fair held in commemoration of the death of Moḥammad; — a spiritualistic gathering, or kind of revival, at which men caper and dance and roll on the ground in a state of religious frenzy
v.n. To be concluded, to come to an end; to be all over
v.n. To be finished; to be worn or tired out; to be brought to the verge of death or destruction; to come to an end, to die
'To repeat the words qul, huwaʼllāh, &c., or Say, God is One'; — to grumble (the bowels; see s.v. aṅtrī); to be hungry.

def: s.f. A kind of pulse or vetch, Madras horse-gram, Dolichos uniflorus, or D. biflorus
s.m. pl. (of qalb, q.v.), Hearts, &c.
s.m. A lump of earth or clay, a clod.

adj. See s.v. kul. s.m. The first word of sūras 112, 113, and 114 of the Qorʼān (beginning respectively with the verses qul, huwaʼllāhǒ aḥaduṉ, 'Say, God is One'; qul, aʻūẕu bi-rabbiʼl-falaqi, 'Say, I seek the protection of the Lord of the Daybreak'; and qul, aʻūẕu bi-rabbiʼn-nāsi, 'Say, I seek the protection of the Lord of Men'), and used to denote one and all of these sūras or chapters; — reading any one or all of the above-mentioned sūras (this is commonly done at a fātiḥa, q.v., or, by way of benediction, over fruits or sweetmeats presented to the guest at the conclusion of the religious annual feasts held in commemoration of ancestors and other relations); — (hence) conclusion, finish, end
subs: (fr. qul, aʻūẕu, &c.; see above), s.m. One whose practice it is to recite the sūras beginning with the words qul, aʻūẕu for a remuneration in money or food
A fair held in commemoration of the death of Moḥammad; — a spiritualistic gathering, or kind of revival, at which men caper and dance and roll on the ground in a state of religious frenzy
v.n. To be concluded, to come to an end; to be all over
v.n. To be finished; to be worn or tired out; to be brought to the verge of death or destruction; to come to an end, to die
'To repeat the words qul, huwaʼllāh, &c., or Say, God is One'; — to grumble (the bowels; see s.v. aṅtrī); to be hungry.

def: s.m. Clysma, a town in Egypt near Mount Sinai; (for baḥr-ě-qulzum), the Red Sea.
s.m. A cake of unleavened bread (baked in the ashes of a fire); an orbicular plate of wood perforated in the middle for receiving the top of a tent-pole (syn. kulīća or kulća, q.v.)
subs: A broad face, a full, plump face.

def: s.f. = next, q.v.
adj. & s.m. corr. of kumait, q.v. adj. & s.m. Bay-coloured, bay (a horse); — a bay horse.
adj. & s.m. Bay-coloured, bay (a horse); — a bay horse.
s.m. A cake of unleavened bread (baked in the ashes of a fire); an orbicular plate of wood perforated in the middle for receiving the top of a tent-pole (syn. kulīća or kulća, q.v.)
subs: A broad face, a full, plump face.

def: s.f. Aid, assistance, support; reinforcement (of an army); a corps of auxiliaries
subs: To be on the side (of, -kī), to support
(-ko), or kumak karnā (-kī), To aid, help, support; to reinforce.

def: adj. & s.m. Auxiliary; — an ally, aider, supporter.
s.f. contrac. of kūmhal, q.v.
wety: From Sanskrit कोमल (komala).

def: s.m. pl. (of kāmil), The perfect.
wety: From Bengali কুমিল্লা (kumilla).

def: s.m. corr. of kumāć, q.v. s.m. A cake of unleavened bread (baked in the ashes of a fire); an orbicular plate of wood perforated in the middle for receiving the top of a tent-pole (syn. kulīća or kulća, q.v.)
subs: A broad face, a full, plump face.

def: s.f. Bad advice, evil counsel; intrigue.
s.m. (f. -ī), A child, boy, youth; an unmarried youth; a son; the son of a Rājā, a prince, the heir-apparent; — a name of Skanda, the god of war.
s.m. (f. -ikā), s.m. = kumār, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), A child, boy, youth; an unmarried youth; a son; the son of a Rājā, a prince, the heir-apparent; — a name of Skanda, the god of war.
s.m. Bad road or way; wrong course, bad ways, &c. ( = ku-path, q.v.). s.m. Bad road, evil way; bad conduct, evil course of life; — deviation, aberration; immorality; heresy; heterodoxy
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Going in a bad or wrong road. following evil ways, immoral; heretical, heterodox; — One who follows an evil course of life, an immoral man, a profligate; a heretic.

def: s.f. A young girl (one from ten to twelve years old, which is regarded as a marriageable age), maiden, maid, virgin, damsel, lass; a daughter; a princess (see kuṅwārī)
subs: s.f. An unmarried damsel, a maid, virgin, &c. ( = kumārī)
s.f. The murder of a young girl.

def: s.f. = kumārī, q.v. s.f. A young girl (one from ten to twelve years old, which is regarded as a marriageable age), maiden, maid, virgin, damsel, lass; a daughter; a princess (see kuṅwārī)
subs: s.f. An unmarried damsel, a maid, virgin, &c. ( = kumārī)
s.f. The murder of a young girl.

ety: S. कुमार+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. The time or the condition of youth, boyhood, youth, youthfulness; bachelorhood.

s.m. = kumāć, q.v. s.m. A cake of unleavened bread (baked in the ashes of a fire); an orbicular plate of wood perforated in the middle for receiving the top of a tent-pole (syn. kulīća or kulća, q.v.)
subs: A broad face, a full, plump face.

ety: v.n. fr. قمش, or pl. of qamsh, inf. n. of قمش 'to collect hence and thence'
def: s.m. What is collected hence and thence, anything picked up here and there; trifles, things of no value; household furniture; merchandize, goods, stuff; silken cloth; fine linen; close or thick texture (in cloth, paper, &c.); — breeding, manners.
s.f. Erroneous or dangerous opinion (esp. on religious topics), heterodoxy, heresy.

s.f. A weak mind, slow or weak intellect; a vile or base sentiment; perversity; error; indiscretion, stupidity, folly; — adj. Of slow intellect, weak-minded; injudicious; stupid, foolish; vicious, wicked.
s.f. Severity, harshness; anger
subs: (-se), To be angry (with), to rebuke, to treat harshly.

def: s.f. Ill-affectedness, estrangement of affection.
s.m. Family, tribe, caste, fraternity (see kunbā).
s.m. = kumbā or kunbā, q.v. s.m. Family, tribe, caste, fraternity (see kunbā).
s.m. Family, &c. ( = کمبا kumbā, q.v.)
subs: s.m. The support of a family
or kunbā sakernā, or kunbā ikhaṭṭā karnā, To get a large family
or kunbe-dār, s.m. One who has a large family.

def: s.m. (dialec.) Agricultural labour, farming (cf. kumbi).

s.m. The tree Careya arborea (dialec. forms are kumbi, khumbi, kumri, gumar; the bark is used as an astringent in native medicine, and also to make coarse strong cordage; the inner bark is made into fuses for matchlocks).

s.m. A water-pot, pitcher, jar; — the sign Aquarius; — a measure of grain equal to twenty droṇas; the frontal globe on the upper part of the forehead of an elephant; a religious exercise, viz. closing the nostrils and mouth so as to suspend breathing ( = kumbhak)
subs: or kumbh-kā melā, s.m. A fair held by Hindū faqīrs every twelfth years at Hardwār and Allahabad (so called because the sun is then in Aquarius)
(S. kumbha+aṇḍās), s.m. pl. A class of demons with testicles shaped like a kumbha
vulg. kumbhaj, adj. (f. -ā), Born in a water-pot; — an epithet of Agastya
s.f. A bilious affection, a sort of jaundice together with swelling of the joints
s.m. lit. 'Having ears like water-pots'; name of a Rākshas, the gigantic brother of Rāvaṇ.

def: s.m. A particular religious exercise, stopping the breath by shutting the mouth and closing both nostrils with the fingers of the right hand.
s.f. = kūmhal, q.v. s.f. 1˚ = kumbhalā, q.v.; — 2˚ = kumhlāʼī, q.v.; — 3˚ = kūmhal, q.v. s.f. A certain plant the flowers of which are compared to those of the Nauclea cadamba.
s.f. A certain plant the flowers of which are compared to those of the Nauclea cadamba.
s.f. A small jar or pot; a vessel for preserving grain in; a measure of grain (equal to twenty dronas).
adj. Shaped like a water-jar
subs: s.m. Name of a hell (in which the wicked are supposed to be baked like a potter's vessels).

def: s.f. A small pot or pitcher; — a disease of the eyes, a stye.
s.m. A thief.

v.n. (dialec.) = kumhlānā, q.v. v.n. To fade, wither, droop; to become blighted; to faint or sink (as the heart); to be or become dejected or dispirited; — v.t. To cause to wither, &c.; to blight, to blast
subs: v.n. (intens.), To fade away, wither away, &c.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = kumhlāʼī, q.v. s.f. Fading, drooping, withering; taking blight, &c.
s.m. The long-nosed alligator, the crocodile of the Ganges.
s.f. 'The lesser Kumbh,' a fair held every sixth year (see kumbh; — it is also called ardha-kumbh).
s.m. (f. -inī), A tribe or class of cultivators; a man of that class; a cultivator of the soil, a peasant (cf. kurmī).
s.m. & f. The lark.
s.m. = kumeṅḍhiyā, q.v. s.m. A small kind of elephant (written also kumeṅṛhiya, kumeṅṛiyā).
